name: Boiled Danish Squash with Ground Beef
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp cornstarch, dissolved in 2 tsp cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut acorn squash into pieces about 1 1/2 inch size.
2. Use a spoon to scrape out seeds and stringy portions. Peel the green skin off and cut.
3. Cut half of the ginger into thin strips and soak in water to crisp. Chop the remaining ginger, about 1 teaspoon.
4. In a pan, place the chopped ginger and Mixture A. Heat and add ground beef. Stir constantly with chopsticks until the liquid has cooked away.
5. Place the squash pieces in the pan (on top of the beef). Add Mixture B and enough water to almost cover the squash.
6. Place a smaller lid on top of the squash.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil before covering the pan with its own cover.
8. Reduce heat and simmer until tender. Remove the squash, bringing the remaining mixture to a boil. Stir in the cornstarch mixed with water, then cook until thickened.
9. To serve, place the squash on a plate and spoon the ground meat over it. Garnish with thinly cut ginger.
